steps.title: Identify what refutes a universal claim
steps.content: A claim that something is universal fails if even one counterexample exists. Here, the student claims ego-based (speaker-relative) thinking is universal. To weaken this, we need a finding showing that some group uses a non-egocentric system.

steps.title: Find the counterexample
steps.content: Choice A describes the Yupno pointing downhill regardless of which way they face—a fixed geographic reference not tied to the speaker's orientation. The other three either show similarity, leave the system's nature unclear, or address gesture frequency. A non-egocentric system directly contradicts the universality of ego-based thinking. That matches choice A — A is correct.
